title: T-065 — Add quality permission strings to enum (RBAC governance)
prompt:
# T-065 — Add quality permission strings to enum

## Goal
Append a delimited `QUALITY` permission group to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` covering the 13 canonical RBAC strings declared by PRD `docs/prd/09-QUALITY-PRD.md` (§2.3 RBAC matrix, §6.3 NCR, §6.3 HACCP, §8 QA-031A inspection.assign, §8 QA-060 settings admin, §2.2 auditor 7y export). Settings/Auth owns the enum file; this task only contributes a new namespaced section under the `quality.*` prefix. Export `ALL_QUALITY_PERMISSIONS` so the ESLint enum-lock guard (02-settings T-130) recognizes the module group.

## Implementation contract
- File: `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` — append entries to `Permission` const object with JSDoc citing the PRD anchor. Do NOT remove or rename any existing string.
- File: `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` — extend the suite to assert presence, regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, uniqueness, and `ALL_QUALITY_PERMISSIONS` typed `readonly Permission[]` with length === 13.
- File: `CODEOWNERS` — verify `/packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts @monopilot/architect` line exists; if missing, add it (already present today).

## Permission strings (13)
- `quality.hold.create`
- `quality.hold.release`
- `quality.spec.approve`
- `quality.inspection.execute`
- `quality.inspection.assign`
- `quality.ncr.create`
- `quality.ncr.close_critical`
- `quality.ccp.deviation_override`
- `quality.haccp.plan_edit`
- `quality.batch.release`
- `quality.dashboard.view`
- `quality.settings.edit`
- `quality.audit.export`

## Files in scope
- `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` [modify]
- `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` [modify]
- `CODEOWNERS` [modify if missing entry]

## Acceptance criteria (Given-When-Then)
- AC1 — Given the enum file is imported, when the consumer reads the new keys, then all 13 strings listed above are present exactly once.
- AC2 — Given each new permission string is matched against regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, when tested, then every string passes and zero duplicates exist across the union of `Object.values(Permission)`.
- AC3 — Given the module export, when imported, then `ALL_QUALITY_PERMISSIONS` is exported as typed `readonly Permission[]` with length === 13.
- AC4 — Given `CODEOWNERS` is read, when checked, then `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` is architect-only (line `/packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts @monopilot/architect` present).

## Test strategy (RED first, vitest)
1. Extend `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` with the four ACs above. Run RED:
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts`
   — must fail before any enum change.
2. Implement minimum changes in `permissions.enum.ts`.
3. Re-run vitest — must pass.
4. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in the changed package; both must pass.

## Risk red lines
- Do not weaken the regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` or introduce a 4-segment / matrix permission model.
- Do not introduce a parallel permission model (no per-module `*PermissionEnum` in another file).
- Do not remove or rename existing strings.
- Do not edit permission strings owned by other modules.
- Note: allergen dual-sign permissions are owned by 08-PRODUCTION (`production.allergen_gate.sign_{first,second}`) — do NOT re-declare them here.
- Do not commit secrets or vault keys.

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact RED→GREEN test commands and outputs
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

pipeline_inputs.description: Add 13 `quality.*` permission strings to packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts and export ALL_QUALITY_PERMISSIONS; closes RBAC enum-lock gap originally mis-anchored to 09-quality T-037 (which is the ncr_reports schema task, not the perm-enum task). F10 reconciliation 2026-05-14.
pipeline_inputs.details: PRD anchors corrected: §6.4→§6.3 and §6.5→§6.3 by Fixer F12 2026-05-14 (anchor sweep; §6.4/§6.5 do not exist — NCR and HACCP entity definitions are in §6.3 Key table summaries). Strings (count=13): ['quality.hold.create', 'quality.hold.release', 'quality.spec.approve', 'quality.inspection.execute', 'quality.inspection.assign', 'quality.ncr.create', 'quality.ncr.close_critical', 'quality.ccp.deviation_override', 'quality.haccp.plan_edit', 'quality.batch.release', 'quality.dashboard.view', 'quality.settings.edit', 'quality.audit.export']. PRD source: §2.3 RBAC matrix lines 77-85 + §6.4 NCR close_critical dual-sign + §6.5 HACCP plan_edit + §8 QA-031A inspection.assign + §8 QA-060 settings admin + §2.2 auditor 7y export. Note: allergen dual-sign is owned by 08-PRODUCTION (production.allergen_gate.sign_{first,second}) — not duplicated here per cross-module decision. Same conventions as 02-settings T-001/T-002: const object with JSDoc per entry citing the PRD anchor, typed `Permission` union via `(typeof Permission)[keyof typeof Permission]`, and `ALL_QUALITY_PERMISSIONS` declared `as readonly Permission[]`.
out_of_scope: Do not introduce a 4-level matrix permission model.
Do not edit other modules' permission strings.
Do not move permissions into Reference.RolePermissions table (enum is authoritative; the table is only the role→permission assignment mapping).
Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components.
Do not declare production.allergen_gate.sign_{first,second} here — owned by 08-PRODUCTION.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Settings owns the enum file; this task appends a delimited namespaced group following the T-001 pattern.

ESLint enum-lock guard (`no-direct-permissions-enum-edit`) registered in packages/rbac/.eslintrc.cjs recognises module groups via ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS exports — this task adds the corresponding ALL_QUALITY_PERMISSIONS export.

acceptance_criteria: All 13 permission strings listed in pipeline_inputs.permission_strings are present in packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts, with JSDoc citing the PRD anchor.
Every new string matches regex ^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$ and no duplicates exist across Object.values(Permission); vitest assertion enforces both.
ALL_QUALITY_PERMISSIONS is exported from packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts as `readonly Permission[]` with length === 13; type-check passes.
test_strategy: RED: extend packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts with presence + regex + uniqueness + array-length assertions for the 13 new strings. Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts`; expect failure.
GREEN: append the strings + ALL_QUALITY_PERMISSIONS export; re-run vitest; expect pass.
Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` after the change; both must pass.
risk_red_lines: Do not introduce a parallel permission model (no per-module *PermissionEnum file).
Do not remove or rename existing strings.
Do not edit permission strings owned by other modules.
Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components or table rows.
